label: Finger tendinitis without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated finger tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed finger tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, fracture, infection, nerve or blood-flow problems, or another hand emergency today.
home: Rest from activities that make finger pain worse.
Use ice, splinting, gentle motion, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to gripping and repetitive finger use gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue or pale fingers, or trouble moving the finger.
A pop, new deformity, inability to bend or straighten the finger, or severe worsening pain.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, new injury, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, hand clinic, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated finger tendinitis.
No tendon rupture, hand tendon injury, fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed hand plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, hand tendon injury, fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, inability to use the limb, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated finger tendinitis with rupture, fracture, wound, infection, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated finger tendinitis

label: Elbow tendinitis without rupture or neurovascular red flags

found: Your clinician diagnosed elbow tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, fracture, elbow instability, nerve or blood-flow problems, infection, or another elbow emergency today.
home: Rest from activities that make elbow pain worse.

meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.

return: New numbness, weakness, blue or pale fingers, or trouble using the hand.

exclusion: Tendon rupture, distal biceps or triceps concern, fracture or dislocation concern, elbow instability, ulnar nerve symptoms, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, inability to use the limb, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.

summary: Your symptoms fit uncomplicated foot tendinitis.
found: Your clinician diagnosed foot tendinitis. We did not find tendon rupture, fracture, infection, poor blood flow, or diabetic-foot red flags today.
home: Rest from activities that make foot pain worse.

return: A pop, new deformity, inability to walk, or severe worsening pain.

exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, diabetic foot, PVD, neuropathy, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed podiatry/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise.
